turns.name: JOHN ROBERTS
turns.text: Justice Alito has our opinion in case 21-454, Sackett versus the Environmental Protection Agency.

turns.name: SAMUEL ALITO
turns.text: This case involves the outer reaches of the Clean Water Act. The Clean Water Act applies to “navigable waters”, which it defines as “the waters of the United States”. The Environmental Protection Agency asserted jurisdiction over Michael and Chantall Sackett’s slot near a lake in Idaho, because the agency believes that the property contains wetlands and constitutes part of the waters of the United States. The Sacketts disagreed and they sued to preclude enforcement. After seven years of litigation, which included a prior trip to this Court, the district court eventually entered summary judgment in favor of the EPA, and the Ninth Circuit affirmed. For the reasons explained in our opinion, we agree with the Sacketts and therefore reverse. We hold that the waters of the United States encompass only those relatively permanent standing or continuously flowing bodies of water forming geographical features that are described in ordinary speech as streams, oceans, rivers and lakes. The opinion of the court is joined in full by the Chief Justice and Justices Thomas, Gorsuch and Barrett. Justice Thomas has filed a concurring opinion, which Justice Gorsuch has joined. Justice Kagan has filed an opinion concurring in the judgment, which Justices Sotomayor and Jackson have joined. Justice Kavanaugh has filed an opinion concurring in the judgment, which Justices Sotomayor, Kagan, and Jackson have joined.
